_meta.notes: Client profile + 3 stakeholders. Content lifted verbatim from the old scenario page (Card 3).

pageTitle: All My Eggses Live in Texas | IS2053 Programming I

card.topperTitle: All My Eggses Live in Texas

aboutTheClient.label: About the Client
aboutTheClient.body_html:
All My Eggses Live in Texas is an eccentric family-owned breakfast restaurant chain with 47 locations across Texas. Founded by Emmett Hutchins III, they’re famous for their over-the-top Texas pride, punny menu items, and dad-joke-fueled marketing.

They’ve hired Bat City Collective to build a promotional video game that captures the spirit of Texas road trips. They want something their customers can play on the tableside kiosk systems while waiting for their “Eggs-traordinary Breakfast Platter.”

Their goal? Customer engagement, brand awareness, and sharing their love of Texas with the world.

stakeholders.name: Emmett Hutchins III
stakeholders.quote_html: “Make it feel like Texas! I want people to taste the brisket through the screen!”
stakeholders.bio_html: Third-generation Texan. Built the restaurant empire from a single food truck. Believes everything is better with Texas flag wallpaper. His enthusiasm is infectious, if overwhelming.

stakeholders.name: Tammy Jo Hutchins-Miller
stakeholders.quote_html: “Our customers will love this! Make sure it’s shareable on social media, okay?”
stakeholders.bio_html: Emmett’s daughter. MBA from UT Austin. She’s the reason they have an Instagram presence and why their hashtag game is strong. Practical, data-driven, but plays along with Dad’s vibe.

stakeholders.name: Bobby Hernandez
stakeholders.quote_html: “Keep it simple and reliable. If it crashes, we lose customers.”
stakeholders.bio_html: The only person at All My Eggses who understands what an API is. Former IT manager at H-E-B. Practical voice of reason who keeps Emmett’s wilder ideas grounded in reality.
